mnemonics: DAD makes tea: Difference to Achieve Stationarity, then Determine ARIMA(p,d,q) parameters.
keyPoints: Nonstationary series require differencing to remove trends and achieve stationarity before modeling.
An ARIMA(p,d,q) model incorporates differencing (d) to handle nonstationarity, combined with AR (p) and MA (q) components.
The order of differencing (d) is chosen as the minimum number of differences required to make the series stationary.
commonMistakes: Applying an ARMA model directly to a nonstationary series without first differencing.
Over-differencing a series, which can introduce unnecessary correlation and weaken the model.
Confusing the differencing parameter (d) in ARIMA with the order of the AR (p) or MA (q) components.
